name: Tortellini with Goat Cheese and Scallions
recipeIngredient: 2 Cups soft goat cheese (preferably Coach Farm)
1/2 Cup grated Parmigiano-Reggiano (plus 1/4 Cup for serving)
recipeInstructions:
1. Bring 6 quarts of water to a boil and add 2 tablespoons salt.
2. In a large mixing bowl, mash the goat cheese until soft. Add the thinly sliced scallions, eggs, nutmeg, and grated Parmigiano-Reggiano, and stir well.
3. Using a pasta roller, roll out the pasta to the thinnest setting and cut sheets into 2-inch squares.
4. Bring chicken stock to a simmer in a 4-quart saucepan.
5. Place 1 teaspoon of goat cheese filling in the center of each 2-inch square.
6. Fold opposite corners together to form a triangle, pressing edges firmly to seal.
7. Bring the opposite points of the triangle together and meld them together with firm finger pressure.
8. Repeat until all pasta is done.
9. Drop tortellini into boiling water and cook until tender (about 7-10 minutes). Drain well.
10. Return tortellini to simmering chicken broth, add reserved scallions and remaining grated Parmigiano-Reggiano, check for seasoning, and serve immediately.
Yield: 4 servings
